title: Red Bull Ring
sub: Short, sharp and savage: ten corners crammed into a mountain amphitheatre. Big elevation, heavy braking, and one of the easiest tracks to overtake on.

sectors.name: Sector 1
sectors.char: Climb from the main straight up to Turn 1, then the steep haul to the Turn 3 braking zone.

sectors.name: Sector 2
sectors.char: Fast downhill flow through Turn 4, the Turn 6 right, and the long blast that follows.

sectors.name: Sector 3
sectors.char: Quick kinks and the final corners that fire cars back onto the start/finish straight.

tyreAlloc: Softest in the range · C3 / C4 / C5
stratNote: Abrasive, old asphalt + repeated heavy braking hammers the rear axle. Thermal degradation decides it; track temp swings it between one and two stops.
spots: Uphill heavy braking from ~330 to ~85 km/h after a DRS straight. The big one.
Downhill right after another DRS zone; switchback territory.
Brake uphill off the main straight + DRS. Opening-lap chaos common.
otNote: Three DRS zones on a 4.3km lap with seven braking events makes Spielberg a slipstream playground. Track limits at the exits are the real drama.
corners: Hard braking off the main straight into a climbing right. Classic opening-lap spot.
Quick right continuing the steep climb.
The lap’s biggest stop: ~330 to ~85 km/h uphill at over 5g. Prime overtaking.
Long right that tips downhill, opening the fast middle sector.
Fast left kink taken near flat.
Sweeping right onto the run toward the final sector.
Quick left leading into the closing complex.
Medium right setting up the final two corners.
Fast right feeding the last corner.
Final right that launches cars onto the start/finish straight and DRS.
geo: Set in the Styrian hills, the lap climbs hard out of Turn 1 to the highest point after Turn 3, then tumbles down. A 63m swing.
Just 10 corners over 4.318km. Lap times dip into the 1:04s, the quickest on the calendar.
~79% of the lap is full throttle down the long straights, punctuated by hard stops. Low-to-medium downforce.
Owned by Red Bull since 2011 and rebuilt from the old A1-Ring. A sea of orange for Verstappen.
